title: EXIT trap deletes preserved state files if install fails mid-swap
reasoning: PRESERVE_DIR is created inside TMP_DIR (line 148). The EXIT trap (line 125) unconditionally removes TMP_DIR. If the script fails between 'rm -rf $INSTALL_DIR' (line 158) and successful tar extraction (line 160)—e.g., disk full, tar corruption, or set -e triggered by mkdir failure—bash exits, the trap fires, and PRESERVE_DIR containing the user's .env and prism.db is permanently deleted alongside the already-removed INSTALL_DIR. The user loses both their running install and their backed-up state in one failure.
reproduction: Fill the target filesystem to capacity after the checksum passes, then run the installer over an existing install that has a .env and prism.db. mkdir -p or tar -xzf fails, set -e exits, the EXIT trap removes TMP_DIR (including PRESERVE_DIR), and the original INSTALL_DIR was already deleted on line 158.
recommendation: Place PRESERVE_DIR outside TMP_DIR (e.g., a sibling mktemp directory) and give it its own trap that restores files to INSTALL_DIR on failure, or restructure the swap to extract into a staging directory and only move the old INSTALL_DIR aside after the new extraction succeeds (rename-based atomic swap).
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for this feature; the failure requires simulating a mid-install abort after INSTALL_DIR removal but before tar extraction completes.
suggestedRegressionTest: Shell test: create a fake INSTALL_DIR with .env and prism.db, stub tar to exit 1, run install.sh, assert the .env and prism.db still exist on disk after the script exits non-zero.
minimumFixScope: Move PRESERVE_DIR to a separate mktemp -d outside TMP_DIR; update the EXIT trap to restore preserved files into INSTALL_DIR if the script exits non-zero before restoration completes.
